# Monk Class Feats — Player Core 2 (PF2e Remastered)

Complete reference of all Monk class feats from Player Core 2 (PC2), levels 12–20.

Source: [Archives of Nethys](https://2e.aonprd.com/) — Player Core 2 pg. 124–128

---

## Level 12 (7 feats)

### Disrupt Qi
- **ID:** 6024 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Monk, Void
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 124
- **Description:** You carefully target a creature's pressure points to block its inner life force. Make an unarmed Strike. If it deals damage to a living creature, that creature takes 2d6 persistent void damage and is enfeebled 1 until the persistent damage ends. If you're 18th level or higher, the persistent void damage increases to 3d6.

### Dodging Roll
- **ID:** 6025 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 124
- **Prerequisites:** Master in Acrobatics
- **Trigger:** You take damage from an area effect that allows a Reflex save.
- **Description:** You throw yourself to the side to avoid the worst of the area effect. You Step and gain resistance to all damage equal to your level against the triggering effect. If your Step moves you out of the triggering effect's area, this resistance is instead equal to your level plus your Dexterity modifier.

### Focused Shot
- **ID:** 6026 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Concentrate, Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 124
- **Prerequisites:** Monastic Archer Stance
- **Requirements:** You are in Monastic Archer Stance.
- **Description:** Make a ranged weapon Strike against a target within your weapon's first range increment. This Strike ignores the target's concealed condition and all cover.

### Improved Knockback
- **ID:** 6027 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 124
- **Prerequisites:** Knockback Strike
- **Description:** When you successfully Shove a creature, increase both the distance you can push the creature and the distance you can move to follow along with the target by 5 feet on a success or 10 feet on a critical success. If you push the target into another creature or obstacle, they both take 1d6 bludgeoning damage per 5 feet of movement that was prevented.

### Meditative Focus
- **ID:** 6028 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 124
- **Prerequisites:** Qi Spells
- **Description:** When you Refocus, you regain all your Focus Points instead of 1.
- **Leads To:** Meditative Wellspring (legacy)

### Overwhelming Breath
- **ID:** 6029 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Concentrate, Monk, Spellshape
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 125
- **Prerequisites:** Qi Spells
- **Description:** If you use your next action to Cast a Spell that has the monk trait and no duration, the spell and any Strikes you make as a result of casting it ignore an amount of the target's resistance to physical damage equal to your level.

### Reflexive Stance
- **ID:** 6030 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** [F] (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 125
- **Trigger:** You roll initiative.
- **Description:** Use an action that has the stance trait.
- **Leads To:** Master of Many Styles

---

## Level 14 (8 feats)

### Form Lock
- **ID:** 6031 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Attack, Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 125
- **Requirements:** You have a foe grabbed or restrained.
- **Description:** Attempt an Athletics check to counteract a polymorph effect currently affecting your target. If the target is somehow under the effect of multiple polymorph effects, you can choose which one to attempt to counteract; otherwise, the GM chooses randomly. If you succeed, the target reverts to its normal form and can't transform for the rest of the encounter. The counteract rank is half your level rounded up, and the counteract check modifier is your Athletics modifier. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 day.
- **Also:** Wrestler archetype

### Ironblood Surge
- **ID:** 6032 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 125
- **Prerequisites:** Ironblood Stance
- **Requirements:** You are in Ironblood Stance.
- **Description:** You gain the benefits of your iron sweep's parry trait (a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn) and your resistance from Ironblood Stance increases to your Strength modifier (if it's higher) for the same duration.

### Mountain Quake
- **ID:** 6033 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 125
- **Prerequisites:** Mountain Stronghold
- **Requirements:** You are in Mountain Stance.
- **Description:** You stomp, shaking the nearby ground. Creatures on the ground within a 20-foot emanation take damage equal to your Strength modifier (minimum 0), with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC. On a failure, they also fall prone. After you use this action, you can't use it again for 1d4 rounds. **Special** If you have this feat, the Dexterity modifier cap to your AC while using Mountain Stance increases from +1 to +2.
- **Also:** Martial Artist archetype

### Peerless Form
- **ID:** 6034 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Description:** You cease aging, though you still die when your time is up. In addition, you gain a +2 status bonus to Fortitude and Will saving throws.

### Shadow's Web
- **ID:** 6035 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Clinging Shadows Initiate
- **Description:** You can launch a wave of shadowy qi outward from your body, damaging your opponents and potentially binding them in place. You gain the shadow's web qi spell.

### Tangled Forest Rake
- **ID:** 6036 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Tangled Forest Stance
- **Requirements:** You are in Tangled Forest Stance.
- **Description:** Make a lashing branch Strike. If you hit and deal damage, you can Reposition the target up to 10 feet. You can move the target through your space during this movement.

### Whirling Blade Stance
- **ID:** 6037 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Monk, Stance
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Monastic Weaponry
- **Description:** All melee monk weapons you wield with the finesse trait gain the thrown 10 feet trait (unless they already have the thrown trait with a greater range). Once you've made a thrown Strike with such a weapon, you can use the precision of your throw to make additional strikes with it, even from a distance. Start from the space of the previous Strike's target to determine the range increment and whether the new target has cover. At the end of your turn, the thrown weapon flies directly back to you in a straight line. If a solid barrier blocks its path, it falls to the ground after hitting the barrier.

### Wild Winds Gust
- **ID:** 6038 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Air, Concentrate, Manipulate, Monk
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Wild Winds Initiate
- **Requirements:** You are in Wild Winds Stance.
- **Description:** You build air pressure with repeated motions, before releasing an enormous crescent of rushing wind strikes. Make a wind crash Strike against each creature in your choice of a 30-foot cone or a 60-foot line. These attacks all count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you make all the attacks.

---

## Level 16 (5 feats)

### Fuse Stance
- **ID:** 6039 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** At least two stances.
- **Description:** Choose two stances you know and combine them into a single fused stance. Give your new fused stance a unique name. When you enter your fused stance, you gain all the effects of both stances, including the requirements and restrictions. If the stances both grant special attacks, you gain all those attacks. If a stance restricts you to one particular attack, you must abide by that restriction. If the fused stances have incompatible restrictions, the GM determines which apply, or determines you can't fuse those stances at all.

### Master of Many Styles
- **ID:** 4850 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Fighter, Monk
- **Action:** [F] (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** [Monk] Reflexive Stance
- **Trigger:** Your turn begins.
- **Description:** You use an action with the stance trait.
- **Leads To:** Immortal Techniques, Qi Center

### Master Qi Spells
- **ID:** 6040 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** Qi Spells
- **Description:** You gain medusa's wrath, touch of death, or another 8th-rank qi spell you have access to.

### One-millimeter Punch
- **ID:** 6041 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** One-inch Punch
- **Description:** When you damage a target using One-Inch Punch, you can focus your qi to send the foe flying. If you do, the target must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected. **Success** The target is pushed back 5 feet. **Failure** The target is pushed back 10 feet. **Critical Failure** The target is pushed back 10 feet for each action you spent on One-Inch Punch.

### Shattering Strike
- **ID:** 6042 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Description:** Make an unarmed Strike. It bypasses the target's resistances. If the target has Hardness, the Strike treats the Hardness as if it were half its value.

---

## Level 18 (5 feats)

### Diamond Fists
- **ID:** 6043 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Description:** Your unarmed attacks gain the forceful and deadly d10 traits. Any that already had one or both of these traits increase their weapon damage dice by one step.

### Grandmaster Qi Spells
- **ID:** 6044 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** Qi Spells
- **Description:** You gain embrace nothingness, qi form, or another 9th-rank qi spell you have access to.

### Qi Center
- **ID:** 6045 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** [F] (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Frequency:** Once per minute
- **Prerequisites:** Master of Many Styles, Qi Spells
- **Description:** You cast a single action qi spell with the stance trait, without spending a Focus Point.

### Swift River
- **ID:** 6046 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** [F] (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Trigger:** Your turn ends and you have a status penalty to your Speed or are immobilized or slowed.
- **Description:** End one status penalty to your Speed, or end one immobilized or slowed condition affecting you.

### Triangle Shot
- **ID:** 6047 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Concentrate, Fortune, Monk
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** Monastic Archer Stance, Stunning Blows
- **Requirements:** You are in Monastic Archer Stance and wielding a bow usable with that stance.
- **Description:** Make three ranged Strikes against a single target with the required weapon, each using your current multiple attack penalty, and you take an additional –2 penalty. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty, and you combine the attacks' damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. Your Stunning Blows benefit applies to Triangle Shot, even though it isn't a Flurry of Blows. If all three Strikes hit, the target takes 3d6 persistent bleed damage.

---

## Level 20 (5 feats)

### Enduring Quickness
- **ID:** 6048 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Description:** You're permanently quickened. You can use your extra action to Stride or Leap, or to provide one of the actions needed for a High Jump or Long Jump.

### Godbreaker
- **ID:** 6049 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** Crushing Grab, Whirling Throw
- **Requirements:** You have a creature grabbed or restrained.
- **Description:** You hurl the creature 20 feet into the air, following behind it with a powerful jump, and then make an unarmed Strike. If successful, repeat up to two more times (20 feet up each time). If you miss any Strike, Godbreaker and the grab end, and both you and the opponent fall, taking falling damage. If all three Strikes are successful, you grab the creature and bring it crashing down, dealing your unarmed Strike damage plus falling damage. You land on your feet adjacent to the creature, take no falling damage, and the creature remains grabbed or restrained.
- **Also:** Wrestler archetype

### Immortal Techniques
- **ID:** 6050 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 127
- **Prerequisites:** Master of Many Styles
- **Description:** The first time each round that you use an action that has both the monk and stance traits, you gain 20 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of your next turn.

### Impossible Technique
- **ID:** 6051 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Fortune, Monk
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 128
- **Trigger:** An enemy's attack hits you or you fail a saving throw against an enemy's ability.
- **Requirements:** You are not wearing armor and are not fatigued.
- **Description:** If the triggering effect was an enemy's attack hitting you, the enemy rerolls the attack roll and uses the lower result. If the triggering effect was you failing a saving throw, you reroll the saving throw and use the higher result.

### Lightning Qi
- **ID:** 6052 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** [F] (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 128
- **Frequency:** Once per 10 minutes
- **Prerequisites:** Qi Spells
- **Description:** If your next action is to cast a qi spell that takes at least two actions to cast, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

---

## Non-PC2 Monk Feats (Levels 12–20)

The following Monk feats are from other remaster-compatible or legacy sources and are valid options for Monk characters. They are NOT reprinted in Player Core 2. All are Legacy Content unless otherwise noted.

### Level 12

#### Sense Ki
- **ID:** 2730 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight! pg. 75
- **Prerequisites:** Ki spells
- **Description:** You can read the presence of ki in your area. You gain imprecise lifesense out to a range of 30 feet.
- **Spoilers:** Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Adventure Path

### Level 14

#### Explosive Death Drop
- **ID:** 2271 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Fire, Monk
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** PFS Guide pg. 118
- **Prerequisites:** Blazing Talon Surge
- **Requirements:** You are in Rain of Embers Stance and have a target grabbed or restrained.
- **Description:** You lift your enemy into the air before bringing them crashing down with a fiery detonation. Roll an Athletics check against your target's Fortitude DC. You take a –1 circumstance penalty to your check if the target is one size larger than you and a –3 circumstance penalty if it's larger than that. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check if the target is one size smaller than you and a +3 circumstance bonus if it's smaller than that. **Critical Success** The target takes 12d6 fire damage and falls prone in an unoccupied adjacent square of your choice. The target is no longer grabbed. The target and any creature adjacent to it also each take 1d6 persistent fire damage. **Success** As critical success, but the target takes 6d6 fire damage instead of 12d6, and creatures don't take persistent fire damage. **Failure** The target is no longer grabbed. **Critical Failure** As failure, but you fall prone.

### Level 16

#### Flinging Blow
- **ID:** 1756 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Advanced Player's Guide pg. 130
- **Description:** You take a moment to center yourself, then unleash a powerful blow that sends your target tumbling away. Make an unarmed Strike. If you hit, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or be pushed up to 10 feet directly away from you (or 20 feet, on a critical failure) and fall prone. If another creature or physical object would prevent this movement, both your target and the blocking creature or object take 1d6 bludgeoning damage per 5 feet of movement that was prevented.

#### Electric Counter
- **ID:** 2732 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Electricity, Evocation, Monk
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight! pg. 75
- **Prerequisites:** Wild Winds Initiate
- **Trigger:** You would take damage.
- **Requirements:** You are in Wild Winds Stance.
- **Description:** You gain resistance to electricity equal to your level until the start of your next turn. If the triggering damage was electricity, this resistance applies to it. If the triggering damage was from a melee attack, the attacker takes 3d6 electricity damage. **Special** This feat has the trait corresponding to the tradition of ki spells you cast, either divine or occult.
- **Spoilers:** Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Adventure Path

### Level 18

#### Ki Form
- **ID:** 1760 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Advanced Player's Guide pg. 131
- **Prerequisites:** Ki spells
- **Description:** You have mastered a potent technique for using your ki to reinforce your physical form and propel yourself through the air. You gain the ki form ki spell. Increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1.

#### Effortless Reach
- **ID:** 2733 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight! pg. 75
- **Description:** Focusing the powers of your ki to augment your blows, you stretch and lengthen your body in ways that defy logic. Your unarmed attacks gain the reach trait.
- **Spoilers:** Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Adventure Path

### Level 20

#### Deadly Strikes
- **ID:** 1762 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Advanced Player's Guide pg. 131
- **Description:** You have honed your body to attack with lethal focus. Your unarmed attacks gain the deadly d10 trait, or you increase their deadly trait to d10 if the unarmed attack is already deadly with a smaller die size.

#### Golden Body
- **ID:** 1166 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Pathfinder #150: Broken Promises pg. 76
- **Description:** You perfect your body, refining the flex of every muscle and the bend of every joint to maximum efficiency. Your unarmed Strikes gain the deadly d12 trait, and you have fast healing 20, causing you to regain 20 HP at the start of each of your turns provided you have at least 1 HP.
- **Spoilers:** Age of Ashes Adventure Path

#### Endurance of the Rooted Tree
- **ID:** 1244 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Monk
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Pathfinder #156: The Apocalypse Prophet pg. 72
- **Prerequisites:** Wholeness of Body
- **Description:** Just as the Vale of Aroden retains light and life despite inhospitable surroundings, you can sustain yourself regardless of your environment. You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe. In addition, you can cast the wholeness of body ki spell almost effortlessly. You can cast it as a free action that triggers at the start of your turn.
- **Spoilers:** Extinction Curse Adventure Path

#### Vitality-Manipulating Stance
- **ID:** 2756 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Monk, Stance
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain pg. 77
- **Description:** You have learned to attack meridians and pressure points like your rival Shino Hakusa. You gain a vitality blast ranged unarmed Strike that deals 1d4 negative damage, has a range of 30 feet, and has the unarmed and versatile positive traits. Additionally, when you use Stunning Fist, instead of saving against being stunned, you can choose to have the target make a Fortitude save against your class DC with the following results. You must choose to do this before the target rolls its save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected. **Success** The target's muscles become temporarily weaker. For 1 round, the target is enfeebled 2, and any time it attempts to take an action with the manipulate trait, the action is disrupted unless it succeeds at a flat check with a DC equal to 5 + its enfeebled value. **Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute. **Critical Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute, the flat check is DC 11, and the target takes a –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds for the duration.
- **Spoilers:** Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Adventure Path

---

## Summary

| Level | PC2 Feats | Non-PC2 Feats | Total |
|-------|-----------|---------------|-------|
| 12    | 7         | 1             | 8     |
| 14    | 8         | 1             | 9     |
| 16    | 5         | 2             | 7     |
| 18    | 5         | 2             | 7     |
| 20    | 5         | 4             | 9     |
| **Total** | **30** | **10**    | **40** |

---

*Generated from Archives of Nethys (2e.aonprd.com). 30 PC2 feats (levels 12–20) with complete descriptions. 10 non-PC2 feats listed with full descriptions. Companion file to monk-feats-pc2.md (levels 1–10).*
